Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Quash Subpoena For Seton Medical Center And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: April Glatt & Derek Myers for (respondents) Victoria Salfiti & Samia Salfiti. Thomas Lalanne & Elizabeth Rhoades for Samir & Osama Salfiti.

The Court stated that we are present for a variety of matters and asked what progress had been made regarding the underlying petition. Mr. Lalanne reported that there are outstanding subpoena's, that they have not gotten anything in regards to discovery, that Dr. Stein states that it would be difficult to conduct an examination without the documents and that they have made no progress. Ms. Glatt stated that the petitioners have no standing, that the case should not have even gotten to this point, that the whole intent of estate planning is to avoid this, that the issue is the capacity of Ms. Salfiti at the time the documents were signed, that Ms. Salfiti knows what she is doing and what was done at the time of signing, that the petitioners have not been able to prove or plead anything, that Osama conceded that he has no standing and that this undermines the intent of estate planning. The Court stated that an option is freezing the situation and letting the litigation finish and asked if we can simply rule without the opportunity for a psychiatric exam. Ms. Glatt stated that it could, that the petitioners must have a legitimate reason to doubt her capacity at the time of signing, that Ms. Salfiti resigned as trustee due to the stress brought on by her sons, and that the petitioners are unable to plead a legitimate claim. Mr. Lalanne stated that at the time the trust was executed it was signed by people other than Suheil and Victoria, that she has turned over the operation of the trust to her daughter who has ulterior motives, that she would need to prove competency to prove her actions, that it is wrong that they are being denied access to Dr. Jones records used to support his declaration, that they are being denied discovery, that they have not been given a chance to review documents and cross-examine Mr. Smith, questioned as to how Ms. Salfiti could understand the trust document when it is written in English and she needs an Arabic interpreter for her deposition and that they would like the chance to cross-examine her regarding her deposition. Ms. Glatt stated that Mr. Smith speaks fluent Arabic, that it cannot be assumed that Ms. Salfiti does not read English, that they are following the legal rights of their client, that Ms. Salfiti is under financial strain, that Osama continues to collect the rents, that this is a massive financial detriment and that Ms. Salfiti intentionally disinherited Osama. Mr. Myers stated that the documents being requested are privileged and that the petitioners present no compelling or conclusive evidence as to why they should have it. Mr. Lalanne stated that there was no response to Mr. Blanco's declaration regarding the signatures, that there is support for necessitation of viewing Mr. Smith's files, time records, notes and correspondence regarding what was wanted in the will and trust documents, that it is a very unusual and complicated trust agreement and that they are entitled to the examination of Mr. Smith and Dr. Jones. Ms. Rhoades cited cases and explained why they apply/do not apply and clarified that they are just attempting to elucidate the facts and that there is no intent to insult or imply that Ms. Salfiti does not speak English. Ms. Glatt stated that they object to the declaration of Mr. Blanco, that they do not believe it is valid, defended the selling of a property, and that they have acted with substantial justification. The Court requested that the parties exchange proposed order by 9/1/15, that proposed orders be submitted to the court by 9/4/15 and that the matters will be under submission as of that date. Counsel may submit a 1-2 page document with objections to the orders.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. Maximillian Contreras, CSR #13876, 415-421-3021 (Reported)

Petition Pursuant To Probate Code 17200 For Orders:1.Compelling An Accounting 2.Compelling Delivery
Off Calendar Apr-06-2015 Continued To Apr-20-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas LaLanne for Osama Salfiti. Elizabeth Rhodes for Samer Salfiti. April Glatt & Derek Myers for Victoria Salfiti & Samia Salfiti. Ed Aranki (arabic translator) also present.

Ms. Glatt stated that Osama has no standing and that she rejects to the standing of Samer, that Mrs. Salfiti is still living and has the power to divest interest of anyone she wants as well as the power to sell the properties. Ms. Glatt reported that the loan has been called, that the property has been run into the ground, that they had a buyer but the buyer pulled out and that if they cannot sell the property to make the loan payment they will lose everything. Ms. Rhodes objected to the appearance of the translator, that he is not court appointed/certified, that the trust is invalid, that trust documents are being hidden and that the request to evaluate competency was explained in the request for psychiatric evaluation. Mr. LaLanne stated that if Victoria executed the trust without testamentary authority Samer would have standing, that they are attempting to do discovery, that they are trying to subpoena bank and medical records and that there are motions pending to try to quash this effort. Ms. Glatt stated that they have no standing to bring this request, that the settlor is here in court and can answer any questions and cited Probate Codes 8.10, 8.11 & 8.12 regarding her capacity. Ms. Rhodes stated that they have arguments based on the validity of this trust, that they have been trying to obtain further evidence but are stopped at every point, that the trust itself was not produced until the first Lis Pendens was filed and that the former estate attorney for Mr. Salfiti says that Mr. Salfiti never wanted a trust constructed. Ms. Glatt stated that the petitioners provided no supporting evidence when they filed their petition, that they have only submitted a declaration from the former attorney, that the attorney acknowledges that he had not been working with Mr. Salfiti at the time the trust was constructed and that with the settlor still alive Osama and Samer have no standing. The court stated that the motions need to be heard before the question of standing can be addressed and asked if the parties would agree to mediation. Mr. LaLanne that they are open to mediation. Ms. Glatt indicated that they are willing to converse, agreed that they did not qualify for pro bono mediation and stated that there is currently no cash, only real 2properties, that there are no funds available for mediation. Mr. LaLanne also stated that he is just hearing about the loan issue today, that it is a private lender and that it is his understanding that the loan would be renewed upon request. Ms. Glatt stated that there are no funds to refinance, efforts are being made to sell the property and that there have been issues with Osama not allowing an inspection to go forward. The court requested that the parties meet and confer to see what resolution can be reached regarding the properties and that both sides need to agree on how to attend to the properties. The petition pursuant to probate code 17200 etc., was continued to April 20, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the properties and the issue of standing.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Loos. (Not Reported)
